architecture.summary: TRANX is a transition-based neural semantic parser that maps natural language utterances to abstract syntax trees (ASTs) using a grammar-guided transition system. An encoder (bidirectional LSTM) processes the input NL utterance; a decoder (LSTM with attention and parent-feeding augmentation) generates a sequence of tree-construction actions (ApplyConstr, Reduce, GenToken) that incrementally build an AST according to an Abstract Syntax Description Language (ASDL) grammar. The generated AST is then converted to a domain-specific meaning representation (MR) via a user-supplied AST_to_MR() function. Parent feeding concatenates the embedding of the parent frontier field with the decoder state at the parent's construction to model AST topology.

biblio.title: TRANX: A Transition-based Neural Abstract Syntax Parser for Semantic Parsing and Code Generation

claims.statement: Tranx outperforms existing neural network-based approaches on semantic parsing tasks
Tranx achieves state-of-the-art results on Django code generation
Parent feeding yields a +1 point gain in accuracy on Django
Tranx with answer pruning achieves exact match accuracy on WikiSQL
Tranx is highly generalizable and can easily be applied to new types of MR by just writing a new abstract syntax description corresponding to the allowable structures in the MR
The transition system used by Tranx can be easily extended for WikiSQL with minimal engineering

Tranx decouples the semantic parsing procedure from specificities of grammars by employing ASTs as a general-purpose intermediate meaning representation, with task-dependent grammar provided as external knowledge
Model without parent feeding achieves slightly better accuracy on Geo, probably because its relatively simple grammar does not require extra handling of parent information
